title: Restore Vial Traite Service Frontend After Draft Expiration

requirements.summary: Restore complete frontend application with all existing features: authentication, clients management, interventions, technical folder, blacklist management, offline sync, and PWA support
acceptanceCriteria: The app deploys and opens without crashing before the login screen.
Internet Identity authentication works and redirects to the home route after login.
The Clients page lists all clients with search, blacklist badges, and navigation to individual dossiers.
Client dossiers display contact info, blacklist status, and intervention history.
Interventions can be added, edited, deleted, and viewed with media attachments.
The Technical Folder page allows file upload, folder creation, renaming, moving, and viewing files.
The Dashboard page shows a calendar and interventions for the selected date.
Offline sync (outbox, sync engine, connectivity status bar) functions correctly.
PWA manifest and service worker are present and the app is installable.
Data export (JSON/PDF) dialog is accessible and functional.

file_operations.description: Verify TanStack Router setup with authenticated/unauthenticated route trees, QueryClientProvider, ThemeProvider, and AuthenticatedLayout. Ensure useEffect-based navigation guards are in place. Use the authorization component to ensure users must be logged in before seeing application data.

Verify Internet Identity login flow, redirect logic to home route after authentication, and edge case handling (already-authenticated sessions). Ensure the authorization component's frontend usage instructions are followed for login/logout and principal management.

Verify modal dialog prompts newly authenticated users to enter their name on first login using getCallerUserProfile and saveCallerUserProfile from the backend interface. Block dismissal until profile is saved. Follow the authorization component's profile setup prevention flash pattern.

Verify hooks for fetching caller's user profile (getCallerUserProfile) and saving it (saveCallerUserProfile) via backend actor, with toast notifications. Ensure proper loading states to prevent profile setup modal flash as described in the authorization component usage.

Verify page lists all clients with search filter (using searchClients backend method), displays blacklist badges, enables navigation to individual client dossiers, and includes CreateClientDialog. Ensure loading and error states are handled.

Verify dialog form for creating new clients with fields for name, address (street, city, state, zip), phone, and email. Ensure it uses useCreateClient hook which calls createOrUpdateClient backend method.
Verify dialog form pre-populated with existing client data for editing name, address, phone, and email via useUpdateClient hook calling createOrUpdateClient backend method.
Verify React Query hooks for fetching all clients (getClients), fetching single client by ID (getClient), creating client, updating client, and searching clients (searchClients) via backend actor.

Verify page displays full client dossier including contact info (name, address, phone, email), blacklist status with BlacklistPanel, intervention history using InterventionList, and edit dialog. Handle loading/error states.

Verify panel for managing client blacklist status, allowing users to mark or unmark as blacklisted with optional comments and media attachments. Uses markAsBlacklisted and unmarkAsBlacklisted backend methods. Verify the blob-storage component usage for handling media attachments.
Verify mutation hooks to mark (markAsBlacklisted) or unmark (unmarkAsBlacklisted) client as blacklisted via backend actor, with toast notifications and query cache invalidation.

Verify component displays list of interventions for a client with add, edit, delete, and view actions. Uses getClientInterventions backend method. Handle loading/error states and confirmation dialogs for deletion.

Verify dialog for creating new intervention with date fields (day, month, year as bigint), comments textarea, and media picker using MediaPicker component. Uses addIntervention backend method. Verify the blob-storage component usage for media handling and the camera component usage for photo capture.

Verify dialog for editing existing intervention, pre-populating fields from intervention object and submitting via updateIntervention backend method. Verify the blob-storage component usage for media handling.

Verify read-only dialog displaying intervention details (date, employee principal, comments, media) with optional edit button for authorized users (canEdit flag). Uses MediaPreview component for displaying media.

Verify React Query hooks for fetching client interventions (getClientInterventions), adding (addIntervention), updating (updateIntervention), and deleting (deleteIntervention) interventions via backend actor, with toast notifications and cache invalidation.

Verify media management component allowing users to add media via file upload or camera capture (using CameraDialog sub-component) and display thumbnails with remove buttons. Verify the camera component usage instructions are followed: always show camera preview, place capture button on preview, handle camera errors properly, ensure non-zero preview dimensions, make buttons clickable only when camera is ready.

Verify component renders grid of image thumbnails from ExternalBlob objects using getDirectURL() method, and opens DocumentViewer dialog on thumbnail click. Verify the blob-storage component usage for ExternalBlob handling.
Verify button component that opens file input dialog and returns selected files via onSelect callback.

Verify page renders navigable file/folder tree built from flat file paths (from listTechnicalFiles backend method), with support for uploading files, creating folders, and renaming folders. Verify the blob-storage component usage for file operations.

Verify dialog for uploading files to technical folder, supporting both file selection and camera capture with upload progress tracking. Uses uploadTechnicalFileWithFolderPath backend method. Verify the blob-storage component's withUploadProgress method is used for progress tracking and the camera component usage for photo capture.
Verify modal dialog for creating new folder at given path, validating folder name and calling createFolder backend method.
Verify modal dialog for renaming folder, validating new name and calling renameFolder backend method.
Verify modal dialog for moving file to different folder, providing breadcrumb navigation through folder structure and calling moveTechnicalFile backend method.

Verify component renders single file row with view, move, and delete actions, integrating DocumentViewer, MoveFolderDialog, and delete confirmation AlertDialog. Uses deleteTechnicalFileWithPath backend method.

Verify modal viewer for files (images, PDFs, videos) with zoom, rotation, fullscreen, pinch-to-zoom, and download support based on file extension. Uses downloadTechnicalFileWithPath backend method to retrieve file blobs. Verify the blob-storage component usage for ExternalBlob handling.
Verify function returns appropriate Lucide icon component based on file extension, covering images, PDFs, videos, Word, and spreadsheet formats.

Verify React Query hooks for managing technical folder: list files (listTechnicalFiles), upload (uploadTechnicalFileWithFolderPath), delete (deleteTechnicalFileWithPath), move (moveTechnicalFile), create folder (createFolder), rename folder (renameFolder) via backend actor with toast notifications. Verify the blob-storage component usage for ExternalBlob operations.
Verify dashboard page displays calendar and list of interventions for selected date using getInterventionsByDate backend method, with loading/error states and details dialog on item click.

Verify mobile app shell with sticky header (logo from frontend/public/assets/generated/vial-traite-logo.dim_400x200.png, user name from profile, logout button), scrollable main content area, and fixed bottom navigation bar with links to Clients, Dashboard, and Technical Folder pages.

Verify IndexedDB abstraction layer initializes database with 'outbox' and 'cache' stores, exposing helpers to add/get/remove outbox operations and cache arbitrary data with graceful fallback if IndexedDB unavailable.
Verify IndexedDB-backed outbox module for queuing offline operations, providing enqueue, getAll, and remove functions with timestamp index.
Verify sync engine reads queued offline operations and replays them against backend actor, removing successfully processed operations and handling unauthorized errors gracefully.
Verify custom hook tracks online status and pending sync count, automatically triggering sync operation when device comes back online with pending items.
Verify hook tracks and returns browser's online/offline status by listening to window network events.
Verify status bar shows offline state or pending sync operations count, with button to manually trigger synchronization when online.
Verify dialog lets users export application data in JSON or PDF format, showing progress bar during export. Uses useDataExport hook.

Verify hook fetches all clients (getClients), interventions (getClientInterventions for each client), and technical files (listTechnicalFiles) from backend and exports aggregated data as JSON or plain-text file download with progress tracking.

Verify PWA web manifest for 'Vial Traite Service' defines app name, icons (using frontend/public/assets/generated/pwa-icon.dim_512x512.png and pwa-icon-maskable.dim_512x512.png), display mode, theme colors, and current version.

Verify service worker caches PWA assets on install, serves non-navigation requests from cache with network fallback, always fetches navigation requests from network, and cleans up old caches on activate.
Verify function registers PWA service worker (/sw.js) after short delay on page load with basic error logging.

Verify HTML entry point includes French locale, viewport/theme meta tags, manifest link (to manifest.webmanifest), icon links (to generated PWA icons), Apple mobile web app settings, and root div for React app mounting.

Verify global CSS file sets up Tailwind directives and defines CSS custom properties for Vial Traite Service brand color theme in light and dark modes, plus safe-area inset utilities. Preserve existing brand color theme as required by constraints.
Verify Tailwind CSS configuration defines dark mode, custom OKLCH-based color tokens, border radius variables, font stack, and enables typography, container-queries, and animate plugins.
Verify React class-based error boundary catches runtime errors and displays styled error card with options to retry, reload, or copy error details to clipboard.
Verify wrapper around Badge UI component adds custom color variants for 'success', 'warning', and 'pending' states.

Verify route guard component redirects unauthenticated users to login page and shows loading spinner while authentication state is being initialized. Use the authorization component's access control UI guidance.
Verify utility functions for reading URL query parameters (supporting standard and hash-based routing), persisting them to sessionStorage, and cleaning sensitive params from URL hash.
Verify utility module exports cn helper that merges Tailwind class names using clsx and tailwind-merge.
